sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a 2-hour history of worsening low back pain and right foot tingling sensation after raking leaves in the yard.
The pain improved with a hot shower and sitting, but worsened with standing and bending.
The patient reported a similar episode 3 years ago that resolved within a day.
The partner reported similar symptoms, with more severe pain when the patient stands up after bending.
The patient and partner have a history of low back pain after yard work, with the patient's partner experiencing more severe pain after thinking about raking leaves but not actually doing it.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had some pain on flexion, pain on palpation around the L5, and normal dorsiflexion.
Pulses were equal in all extremities.
Test Results: The patient had a normal x-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a probable lumbar strain with possible nerve root irritation.
Problem list: Lumbar strain; possible nerve root irritation; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5mg orally once daily for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve mobility and reduce pain.
I instructed the patient to rest and avoid further heavy lifting or bending for the next two weeks.
I will re-evaluate the patient in two weeks if the symptoms do not improve, and consider an MRI if necessary.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in two weeks to re-evaluate the symptoms and consider further testing if necessary.
